content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 18, 1993

Mary Ann McNett Mason
Deputy County Counsel
County of Contra Costa
P.O. Box 69
Martinez, CA  94553-0116

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-93-418

Dear Ms. Mason:

     This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Delta Diablo Sanitation District Board Member Joel Keller
regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1/  Since you are not seeking advice on a specific
decision before Mr. Keller at this time, we are treating your
advice as a request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

     As an employee of Contra Costa County and a member of the
Contra Costa Public Employees Union No. 1, may Mr. Keller
participate in negotiations and decisions concerning employment
contracts for employees of the District's Maintenance and
Operations Unit which is represented by the Contra Costa County
Public Employees Union Local No. 1?

CONCLUSION

     Neither Mr. Keller's salary from the County of Contra Costa,
nor his membership in the Public Employees Union Local No. 1, are
economic interests under the Political Reform Act. Mr. Keller may
participate in negotiations and decisions concerning District

1/   Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/   Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA  95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Advice File No. I-93-418
Page 2


employment contract for employees represented by the Public
Employees Union Local No. 1.

FACTS

Mr. Keller is a member of the Delta Diablo Sanitation
District (the "District"). The District's Board of Directors will
be considering employment contracts for employees within its
Maintenance and Operations Unit. The Maintenance Unit has contracted with the Contra Costa County Public Employees
Union Local No. 1 (the "employees' union") for assistance in
negotiating those contracts. Mr. Keller is a salaried employee of
the Contra Costa County Probation Department. As an employee of
the county, he is represented by the employees' union.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official positions
to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. An official has a financial interest in a
governmental decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate family, or on:

     (c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status, aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

     (d) Any business entity[3/] in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(Section 87103(c) and (d).)

"Income" as used above does not include salary and
reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a state,
local, or federal government agency. (Section 82030(b)(2).)

Joel Keller is an employee of, and receives a salary from,
Contra Costa County. However, his salary from Contra Costa County

3/ "Business entity" means any organization or enterprise
operated for profit, including but not limited to a
proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture,
syndicate, corporation or association.

Advice File No. I-93-418
Page 3


     is not an economic interest under the Act.  As an employee of the
county, Mr. Keller is also a member of the employees' union.  This
membership is not a business position under Section 87103(d).
There is nothing in the facts to indicate that the union is a
source of income to him.  Since neither Contra Costa County, nor
the employees' union are "business entities" under the Act, Mr.
Keller's status as an employee of the county and a member of the
employees' union does not create the basis for a conflict of
interest.  (Section 82034.)

     Thus, absent some other disqualifying financial interest as
set forth in Section 87103, above, or some direct financial effect
on Mr. Keller personally resulting from a governmental decision,
he may participate in negotiations and decisions concerning
employment contracts for employees of the District's Maintenance
and Operations Unit.

     A decision will have a direct financial effect on a public
official if the decision will result in the personal expenses,
income, assets (other than real property) or liabilities of the
official or his or her immediate family increasing or decreasing
by at least $250.  (Regulation 18702.1(a)(4).)  It does not appear
that the decision to negotiate the employment contracts with
employees of the District will have a direct financial effect on
Mr. Keller.

     Should you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please feel free to contact the legal division at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By: Jeanette E. Turvill
                                            Political Reform Consultant
                                            Legal Division

SGC/JET/jt
